name: José Curbelo
abstract: José Curbelo (February 18, 1917, Havana - September 21, 2012, Miami) was a Cuban-born American pianist and manager. Curbelo was a key figure in Latin jazz in New York City in the 1940s and helped to popularize Mambo and the cha cha dance in the 1950s.

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: Then when I came here to the United States, I met Jose Curbelo, pianist and conductor of the orchestra that bears his name and was working at the Cabaret China Doll.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: José Curbelo, with his Cuban accent, was the agent: 'And then you will have to book him.' He got me 90 gigs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: everytime Varona didn't show up, Jose Curbelo took his place... he say: 'Jose Curbelo.'
